firehoseSSEDisabled.^description: {$COUNT} Amazon Data Firehose delivery stream(s) do not have server-side encryption enabled (DeliveryStreamEncryptionConfiguration.Status is not 'ENABLED'). Without stream-level SSE, records buffered inside Firehose are stored in plaintext, and downstream destinations cannot rely on Firehose for end-to-end key-managed encryption. Aligns with AWS Security Hub control DataFirehose.1.
firehoseSSEDisabled.shortDesc: Enable server-side encryption on every Data Firehose delivery stream

firehoseSSEDefaultKey.^description: {$COUNT} Amazon Data Firehose delivery stream(s) are encrypted with an AWS-owned CMK (KeyType='AWS_OWNED_CMK') rather than a customer-managed CMK. AWS-owned keys satisfy encryption-at-rest but do not permit rotation control, key policy customization, revocation, or cross-account audit — properties often mandated by regulated workloads. Advisory for organisations with CMK controls; can be suppressed when AWS-owned keys are acceptable.
firehoseSSEDefaultKey.shortDesc: Prefer a customer-managed KMS key over the AWS-owned CMK

firehoseS3DestinationNoEncryption.^description: {$COUNT} Amazon Data Firehose delivery stream(s) write to an S3 destination without KMS encryption configured on the destination (EncryptionConfiguration.KMSEncryptionConfig is absent, or NoEncryptionConfig='NoEncryption'). Even if stream-level SSE is enabled, records land in S3 using the bucket's default encryption — configuring an explicit KMS key on the Firehose destination makes the key contract auditable and portable.
firehoseS3DestinationNoEncryption.shortDesc: Configure KMS encryption on Firehose S3 destinations

firehoseLoggingDisabled.^description: {$COUNT} Amazon Data Firehose delivery stream(s) have CloudWatch error logging disabled on at least one destination (CloudWatchLoggingOptions.Enabled=false or absent). Without logging, delivery failures — bad Lambda transformation output, S3 PutObject errors, OpenSearch bulk-write errors, and so on — are invisible: records are retried or dropped with no operator-visible trail.
firehoseLoggingDisabled.shortDesc: Enable CloudWatch error logging on every Firehose destination

firehoseS3BackupDisabled.^description: {$COUNT} Amazon Data Firehose delivery stream(s) have data transformation enabled (ProcessingConfiguration.Enabled=true) but S3 backup of the source records is disabled (S3BackupMode='Disabled'). Without backup, records that fail transformation cannot be recovered to their original form and must be re-emitted by upstream producers. Advisory when data transformation is active.
firehoseS3BackupDisabled.shortDesc: Enable source-record S3 backup when data transformation is used

firehoseBufferingSuboptimal.^description: {$COUNT} Amazon Data Firehose delivery stream(s) buffer S3 output with IntervalInSeconds < 60 or SizeInMBs < 1. Aggressive flushing creates many small objects in S3, which materially increases Athena/Glue scan costs, S3 API costs, and downstream compaction effort. Defaults are 300s / 5MB; tune only when latency requires it.
firehoseBufferingSuboptimal.shortDesc: Increase Firehose S3 buffering interval or size to reduce small-object overhead

firehoseNoTags.^description: {$COUNT} Amazon Data Firehose delivery stream(s) have no user-defined tags applied. Tags enable cost allocation, tag-based IAM, and lifecycle governance.
firehoseNoTags.shortDesc: Tag every Data Firehose delivery stream (owner, environment, cost-centre)

firehoseStreamNotActive.^description: {$COUNT} Amazon Data Firehose delivery stream(s) are in a non-active state (DeliveryStreamStatus in [CREATING_FAILED, DELETING_FAILED, SUSPENDED]). SUSPENDED typically means a KMS key became unavailable and Firehose paused delivery; CREATING_FAILED / DELETING_FAILED indicate the create / delete call hit an unrecoverable error, usually a mis-configured IAM role or destination.
firehoseStreamNotActive.shortDesc: Investigate delivery streams in SUSPENDED / CREATING_FAILED / DELETING_FAILED
